posts.text: Nithin Kamath flags ULIP, endowment traps; — You have just two etf, nifty 50 equal weight which is 2 percent and Nifty 100 equal weight which is 1% allocation. The sensex next 30 gives a higher allocation.

posts.author: cvs
posts.text: Nithin Kamath flags ULIP, endowment traps; — The last bit IS the key point. A single stock cannot affect one’s portfolio. A bunch of all these marginal/incremental reductions in risk put together are expected to completely de-risk a portfolio. Of course, in reality no two assets are completely uncorrelated. There will always be confounding factors. Still, one can minimise the risk/volatility and increase the expected value of one’s portfolio across a wider range of possible scenarios. Update : To be clear, am not saying Nifty50 is well diversified. It (and several other market-cap weighted Ind…

posts.author: neha1101
posts.text: Nithin Kamath flags ULIP, endowment traps; — Thank you very much for the link; I watched it. Regarding the first part ( time stamp from 1:43 onwards), ‘What is wrong with this strategy,’ I agree with what the host is trying to say, but I want to ask: in the Nifty 50, there are approximately 25 stocks—some of them great companies—that have weights of less than 1%. I fully understand that this is how market capitalization works. However, if someone is truly interested in buying these companies, they need to follow a strategy of having satellite ETFs or funds to boost the weights of these low-wei…
